title: Report UI: lab/real-user/best-practices/desktop/UNAVAILABLE rendering

files_forbidden: any scanner module or lib file

acceptance_criteria: The performance card renders: the Lighthouse-accurate headline score/grade (mobile), Google's verbatim real-user verdict beside it, the best-practices grade, INP shown in the Core Web Vitals where present, and an explicit 'performance not measured (provider unavailable)' state when scoreSource==='unavailable' (never a fake F/0, never NaN).
When the chosen score is from lab only (no field data) a clear 'no real-user data for this site' label is shown; the cache-staleness disclosure string is shown where appropriate.

Desktop (when present in the data) renders as a VISUALLY SUBORDINATE section with the disclaimer 'informational — headline grade is mobile (Google's default)'; desktop never shows the HIGH banner, never drives the grade. The 'real users SLOW' banner is mobile + URL-level only (consumes the structure from T-02/T-06; does not re-derive it).

SECURITY: every new CrUX/best-practices string (audit titles/descriptions, verdict text) is rendered ONLY via React's default escaping — NO dangerouslySetInnerHTML on any new field, in both the on-screen report and the PDF/print render path that uses these components. Length is capped at render. A test feeds <script> and javascript: payloads through the new audit/field props and asserts they are escaped (not executed) in the rendered output.
All user-facing strings come from the next-intl catalogs using EXACTLY the key manifest produced by T-05 (no hardcoded English, no missing-key fallback).

Back-compat: a scan whose performanceData has no desktop block / no scoreSource / no field block renders the mobile-only view with no crash and no empty desktop section (covered by a component test with a pre-change fixture).

Component tests cover: field-present, field-absent (lab label), UNAVAILABLE state, desktop-present subordinate render, desktop-absent, XSS payload escaped, old-shape back-compat. npm run lint and npm run test pass.
output_format: code plus a plain-English explanation of the escaping approach, the subordinate-desktop treatment, and the UNAVAILABLE rendering
